title_statement.title: Quality of Life Survey 2023-2024, Round 7 Restricted-Access Data

version_statement.version: v1: Edited, anonymised dataset shared under a CC-BY-NC (non-commercial use only) license
version_statement.version_notes: This version of the Quality of Life 2023-2024 data contains sensitive variables and is shared on receipt of a research proposal on how the sensitive variables will be used in the research. The survey questionnaire is available with the restricted-access data. Users can download the other QOLS 2023-2024 documents from the landing-page of the public access version of this dataset.

study_info.abstract: This dataset is from the Gauteng City-Region Observatory which is a partnership between the University of Johannesburg, the University of the Witwatersrand, the Gauteng Provincial Government and several Gauteng municipalities, including the Gauteng Provincial Government, as well as the South African Local Government Association (SALGA). The GCRO conducted Quality of Life Surveys in 2009 (Round 1), 2011 (Round 2), 2013-2014 (Round 3) and 2015-2016 (Round 4), 2017-2018 (Round 5), and 2020-2021 ( Round 6). Round 7 took place from 11 September 2023 to 19 April 2024 and is the latest round of the survey.
study_info.geog_coverage: The survey covers the Gauteng Province in South Africa.
study_info.geog_unit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation in the data is the Ward level
study_info.universe: The survey covers all adult residents  in Gauteng Province, South Africa.
study_info.notes:
The Gauteng City-Region Observatory's Quality of Life Survey collects demographic data on the enumerated population (population group, gender, age, language) and on housing (dwelling type, tenure, satisfaction with dwelling, perceived quality of housing and housing allocation) as well as household services (water, sanitation, refuse, energy sources). Data is also collected on migration; health (including disability); education; employment (including employment sector); community services and amenities; transport; leisure activities; safety and crime; financial data on households (including on debts, income, and social grants); household assets; public participation and governance ; and the perceived personal well-being and quality of life of respondents.

Additional variables that are available in the restricted-access QOLS 2023-2024 data were collected using Section 15 of the QOLS questionnaire. These questions pertain to respondents' gender identity, sexual orientation, exposure to physical and sexual abuse as a child, experiences of violence over the past year, and perceptions of support with leaving an abusive relationship.

dataset_use.cit_req: Gauteng City-Region Observatory (GCRO). Quality of Life Survey 2023-2024, Round 7 Restricted-Access Data [dataset].Version 1. Johannesburg: GCRO [producer], 2024. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2024. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/0rc4-cf21
